voice_rules.title: First-line discipline
voice_rules.body: First 5 words decide the watch. Pick one shape: a specific number, a vulnerable admission, a pointed take, a named anomaly, or a **viewer-identification question** that names a tension the audience actually feels ("How do you build something new without losing time with your family?", "Anyone else feel like AI is here and you don't know where you fit?"). No setup, no "Hi, today I want to talk about..."

voice_rules.title: Length depends on format
voice_rules.body: Punchy formats (Take / Frame / Reply) reward one sentence per beat — TikTok pace doesn't carry multi-clause sentences. Slower formats (self-intro, Build narratives, reflective Takes 60-75s) reward flowing prose with commas + conjunctions that reads like Dan talking at a dinner party. Don't force fragmentation on a video that wants to breathe.

voice_rules.title: Cut "I think" / "for me" / "personally" as hedges
voice_rules.body: They soften the take. The take is yours by virtue of you saying it. Don't double-flag. **Nuance: "I feel like" is permitted 1–2× per script when it's naming a true felt state ("I feel like I'm at a crossroads") or a real feeling ("I feel like I'll get left behind"). Cut it whenever it's a verbal tic before a claim ("I feel like I've spent my career..." → just "I've spent my career...").**

voice_rules.title: Specifics over scaffolding
voice_rules.body: "5:25am" beats "early morning." "One kid, second on the way" beats "growing family." "Three sessions with Claude" beats "with AI." "Humanitarian photographer for Compassion" beats "humanitarian photographer." "Sold a small clothing company called Borne" beats "started a company once." Name the thing.

voice_rules.title: The close is a plain statement, not a CTA
voice_rules.body: "Posting the work here so I keep going" beats "Follow for more!" **Exception: a vulnerability-earned CTA works when the CTA is paid for by a real admission.** Example (V3 self-intro close): *"I've never really made TikToks before. Follow along if you want to watch me figure this out."* The "follow along" is earned by the prior honesty about being new. Don't use CTAs without earning them.

voice_rules.title: No music carrying the message
voice_rules.body: Voice carries. Music is a bed.

voice_rules.title: Captions match what you say
voice_rules.body: Don't paraphrase yourself on-screen. Either the caption is the same words, or don't caption that line.

voice_rules.title: Dual-tension framing beats one-sided irony
voice_rules.body: When naming the tension you're sitting in, name BOTH forces, not just one. Audience identifies more with named-both than named-one. Example: instead of just *"the irony of less family time while building this isn't lost on me"* (one side), say *"If I don't build now, I'll get left behind. But I don't want to do that at the cost of my family having the best of me."* (both sides, no resolution claimed). The audience feels seen because both forces are theirs too.

voice_rules.title: Emotional pivot lines land plain
voice_rules.body: When a line carries weight ("family having the best of me", "earns while I'm doing bedtime", "posting the work so I keep going"), say it without flourish, then stop. No music swell, no end-card, no rhetorical underline. The line does its own work. Pause slightly before, not after.

voice_rules.title: Tone-true overrides the rule
voice_rules.body: If a phrase is exactly how Dan would say it out loud ("moving so fast", "had a stint as", "Hi, I'm Dan"), keep it even if it edges a voice rule. The rules are heuristics for "sound like you talking." When a rule would make Dan sound less like himself, the rule loses. Use this exception sparingly — it's not a licence to break rules whenever convenient. Test: would Dan actually say this to a friend at a barbecue? If yes, it survives.

voice_rules.title: Quotes are relay, not delivery
voice_rules.body: When the script quotes someone else, treat the line as relay. Say it the way the person said it to you, not the way a voiceover artist would. Don't perform it, don't milk it, don't add a dramatic pause. The "Life moves fast, you'll be lucky to get eighteen, so make them count" line in *05-18-summers* lands because it sits unperformed.

voice_rules.title: End on the sharpest line, not the warmest
voice_rules.body: Discipline outlasts aspiration as a closing register. "And a reminder to know when to leave it" (discipline) beats "I want to be there for all of them" (aspiration). The video earns its weight by refusing the soft landing. Test: does the close add something or just repeat the warmth that's already in the visual? If it repeats, cut it.

voice_rules.title: Don't restate the hook at the close
voice_rules.body: The hook establishes the premise — the count, the stakes, the maths. Restating it at the end is redundant and dulls the close. End on the line that adds, not the line that recaps. (Earlier 18 summers drafts ended on "She's already one. Seventeen left." — true, but a repeat of the hook. The locked close ends on the discipline beat and lets the cut to Rowie carry the count.)

voice_rules.title: Counterpoint visuals
voice_rules.body: When the VO names a person or thing, cut to something else. The viewer does the joining work. Rowie isn't shown in *05-18-summers* until the final hold, after being implied throughout. The counterpoint principle applies to all texture/reflective shapes: don't cut to what you're saying, cut around it.

voice_rules.title: No spoken close on the visual hold
voice_rules.body: The final cut on the kid, the desk, the artefact — let it sit in silence, with music carrying out, or no audio at all. Don't text-overlay your meaning. Don't VO over the hold. The cut IS the close. Resisting the urge to label the meaning is what separates this voice from a productivity reel.

pillars.label: Design / craft
pillars.scope: Type pairing, brand tokens, Figma, design judgement, governance over generation, UI taste calls
pillars.example_subjects: Sweat redesign, Money.com.au, Grays.com.au, a typeface decision, a brand system trade-off

pillars.label: Dad
pillars.scope: Parenting layer, family rhythm, structured freedom in practice
pillars.example_subjects: Rowie (born Oct 2024), Hannah, second kid due September 2026, 5:25am, bath time, the engineered day

pillars.label: Founder
pillars.scope: Building things you own, shipping moments, decisions and trade-offs
pillars.example_subjects: Lia, NOBONK packaging, Voxquote, OpenScripture, robson.studio, Loupe, freelance

pillars.label: Tastemaker
pillars.scope: What good looks like and why. Trains the viewer's eye
pillars.example_subjects: Vignelli, Eames, Eno, 1970s skateboarding, restraint, a building in Newcastle, a typeface, a campaign in the wild

pillars.label: AI / tooling
pillars.scope: How tools actually work in your hand
pillars.example_subjects: Claude (CLAUDE.md, skills folder), Cursor, Tokens Studio, Figma + AI workflows, agentic patterns, Wispr Flow

topics_in_by_pillar.design-craft: Typography, brand systems, UI judgement, governance over generation, design taste calls, real client work anonymised specifically ("a fintech design system rollout"), Figma craft, the senior-designer-with-AI thesis.
topics_in_by_pillar.dad: The 5:25am routine, structured freedom in practice (the day engineered backwards from bedtime), parenting choices (authoritative approach, high warmth high standards), the second pregnancy, what it actually takes to keep deep work and family running together.
topics_in_by_pillar.founder: Building Lia (in-progress, specific decisions, what's hard), NOBONK packaging and brand work (project work, not personal product, per 5 May decision), Voxquote scope generation pipeline, OpenScripture MVP, robson.studio launch, freelance moments, decisions made and trade-offs accepted.
topics_in_by_pillar.tastemaker: A designer Dan respects (and why specifically), a building, a typeface, a campaign in the wild, photography references, Newcastle as place (the light, the pace, specific spots), restraint as a design value, references from outside design (skateboarding, music, architecture).
topics_in_by_pillar.ai-tooling: How tools actually work in your hand: Claude with CLAUDE.md + skills folder, Cursor for coding, Tokens Studio in the design-system loop, Wispr Flow for dictation, Figma + AI workflows, agentic patterns, the prompts and setups that are actually doing work (not abstract takes).

topics_out: Religion / faith deconstruction. Held back until the page has trust (video 8-12 earliest per *02-bench*)
NOBONK as a personal-brand chapter (it's freelance, not the next chapter)
Compassion field photography. Held back until the page has trust
The Lia ICP-specific design-governance take in raw form (sharpen for Lia content separately)
Anything that needs more than 60s to land properly (that's YouTube material, not TikTok)
Hot takes for the sake of hot takes

hook_bank.template: Open with the workflow itself, no setup.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Design / craft, AI / tooling.

hook_bank.template: X wasn't meant to be Y, but that's what makes it iconic." Five-beat: anomaly, origin, name, turning point, cultural moment.

hook_bank.template: I have N days to do M." Numbers do the work.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder, Design / craft.

hook_bank.template: Voice the take you disagree with, then counter from personal experience.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Any pillar.

hook_bank.template: Caption tells the arc, visuals prove. No piece-to-camera monologue. Low-cringe credibility posts.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder, Dad.

hook_bank.template: Open with a memorable external quote, then your read.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Tastemaker, Founder.

hook_bank.template: I'm genuinely terrible at being my own client.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder. Used. Vulnerable-admission shape.

hook_bank.template: How do you build something new without losing time with your family?
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad / AI / tooling. Used (V3 self-intro hook). Viewer-question shape — names the tension the audience is also sitting in.

hook_bank.template: Someone told me once you only get eighteen summers with your kid.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Dad / Founder. Used. Story-led reflective shape — anchors the video in a real moment with a real person, sets up the eight-beat structure (see above). Pattern: "Someone told me once X" → "I asked what he meant" → relayed quote → context → "but the line stuck" → physical artefact → reminder → visual close.

hook_bank.template: What if you could [X]? I wanted to see if it was possible, so I [did X].
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad / AI / tooling / Design. What-if experiment shape — positions Dan as the experimenter, not the expert. Creates an intrigue gap (what was the X? what happened?) without preaching. Works because Dan is genuinely in the middle of figuring something out, not reporting from the other side. Variants: "What if you could build a business while raising kids? I wanted to see if it was possible, so I started." / "What if you could design without ever defaulting to black? I wanted to see if it was possible, so I made the rule."

hook_bank.template: A little reminder of why I'm trying to build [ambition]. And a reminder to know when to [discipline].
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad. Used. Ambition + discipline pairing as a closing beat.

hook_bank.template: I don't want to do that at the cost of my family having the best of me.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad. Used (V3 self-intro emotional pivot). Lands plain, no flourish.

hook_bank.template: Anyone else feel like AI is here and you don't know where you fit?
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / AI / tooling. Considered for V3, not used. Viewer-question shape (felt-experience).

hook_bank.template: Anyone else feel like AI is moving too fast, and you don't know whether to run or wait?
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / AI / tooling. Considered for V3, not used. Viewer-question shape (indecision).

hook_bank.template: Does anyone else feel the AI gold rush happening right now?
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / AI / tooling. Considered for V3, not used. Viewer-question shape (metaphor).

hook_bank.template: I feel a bit silly talking to a camera. Here's why I'm doing it anyway.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad.

hook_bank.template: Hardest thing I've tried: building products that earn while I'm at bedtime.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad. From V3 (original chronological version, preserved).

hook_bank.template: I'm 30. One kid, second on the way. And I just quit being someone else's employee in my head.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / Dad.

hook_bank.template: I'm not scared of failing. I'm scared of stagnating during the biggest tech shift of my lifetime.
hook_bank.fit_notes: Founder / AI / tooling.

hook_bank.fit_notes: AI / tooling / Design.

common_to_hook.common: I'm a UX designer at Endava working on Lia
common_to_hook.hook: Hardest design problem I've taken on: a product for musicians, while still designing for SaaS clients.

common_to_hook.common: AI is changing how I design
common_to_hook.hook: I've used AI on every project this year. Here's what actually changed about the job.

common_to_hook.common: I built a small clothing company
common_to_hook.hook: I sold t-shirts that scared away mosquitoes for two years. Here's what it taught me about brand.

common_to_hook.common: Working on Lia with CQ and Luke
common_to_hook.hook: Three friends, three days off, one question: should we actually do this?

common_to_hook.common: Structured freedom is my operating philosophy
common_to_hook.hook: I'm trying to build a life where what I earn isn't tied to the hours I work. Six months in, here's what's working.

common_to_hook.common: I'm scared about building a company with two kids
common_to_hook.hook: I'm scared of stagnating during the biggest tech shift of my lifetime. So I'm building.
AI generates 200 versions in seconds. Senior design now is choosing which one ships.

common_to_hook.common: Newcastle is a good place to live
common_to_hook.hook: Five reasons I work better in Newcastle than I did in Melbourne.
Up at 5:25. Gym by 5:45. Why I built the day backwards from bedtime.

common_to_hook.common: I'm running my own ideas like client work
common_to_hook.hook: I built my studio site in three sessions after sitting on the Figma for two years. Here's what changed.

signals: Title starts with `tt:` (the established prefix from [[tiktok-workflow]]) or contains "tiktok"
Body mentions hook, video idea, script, post, reel, vlog, or a creator handle

routes.destination: Hook bank section above (`flag: audit-needed` on first pass)
routes.notes: Dan confirms or strikes during Sunday review

routes.destination: `01 Wiki/sources/tiktok-intelligence/inbox/` for the next monthly re-rank
routes.notes: Sits alongside the existing corpus inputs
